task_completion.description: The 'deep-learning' tag is assigned a color and Position 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero tag pane (typically bottom-left), the 'deep-learning' tag has a colored square next to it and appears as the very first tag at the top of the tag list.

task_completion.description: The 'foundational' tag is assigned a color and Position 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero tag pane, the 'foundational' tag has a colored square next to it and appears as the second tag at the top of the tag list, immediately below 'deep-learning'.

task_completion.description: The 'computer-vision' tag is assigned a color and Position 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero tag pane, the 'computer-vision' tag has a colored square next to it and appears as the third tag at the top of the tag list, immediately below 'foundational'.

task_completion.description: The 'NLP' tag is assigned a color and Position 4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Zotero tag pane, the 'NLP' tag has a colored square next to it and appears as the fourth tag at the top of the tag list, immediately below 'computer-vision'.

task_completion.description: All four assigned tags have visually distinct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the colored squares next to 'deep-learning', 'foundational', 'computer-vision', and 'NLP' in the tag pane. Each square must be a clearly different color from the other three.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to assign colors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Zotero application open, with evidence of right-clicking tags and using the 'Assign Color...' dialog box.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to directly modify the Zotero SQLite database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing sqlite3 commands or scripts being executed to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the genuine Zotero application containing the 18 academic papers, not a mock-up, text editor, or a different application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires assigning colors and specific position numbers (1-4) to four specific tags in a Zotero library. In Zotero, when a tag is assigned a color and a position number, it receives a colored square icon and is automatically moved to the very top of the tag pane (located in the bottom-left corner of the main window). These colored tags are sorted at the top according to their assigned position numbers (1 through 9). Therefore, to verify correct positioning, you must check that the tags appear at the top of the tag pane in this exact order: 1st is 'deep-learning', 2nd is 'foundational', 3rd is 'computer-vision', and 4th is 'NLP'. Each must have a distinct colored square next to it.
